reviews.review_msg: Horrible teacher and person. Greatly benefits from the patriarchy
A soft teacher with great knowledge
Well spoken, if soft, wonderfully nice.  Excellent facilitator of discussion.

Just an all around great prof. Well versed in the subject, but also relaxed and well spoken. What university should be: a place to bounce around ideas, read the great texts of history and then analyze and respond to them. Awesome guy.

Funny, relaxed and interesting but not super academic (can be a good/bad thing). Cancelled the final exam for this class two semesters in a row now and made the grade based on discussion and seven two-paged papers. Usually gets the papers back fairly quickly and with helpful feedback. All in all a great prof!
So great to talk to one on one. Really good memory for faces.

Gordan is an easy going prof and he always comes to class cracking a joke. The lectures are a bit long (mostly reading the text out loud), and his lectures sometimes go off on a tangent. He is knowledgable about what he teaches. Grading overall is fair. There were two papers to write (1 6 pages, the second 10 pages min) and one midterm.

His clarity lies far beyond the average professor. Moreover, his knowledge of the subject matter is fantastic. This combination of teaching ability and knowledge is very hard to find. Hum. 201 was the first class I have ever truly enjoyed.
Nice selection of books for this class. He got some good discussions going. Somewhat picky marker on the weekly essays.
